title: Be kind to street dogs.
norm: Don't chase, corner, or throw stones at a street dog. And if any animal bites you, wash it with soap and running water at once, then see a doctor.
wit: A dog that isn't scared has no reason to bite. Same as the rest of us.
goesRight: A dog given room stays calm, fewer people are bitten, and a quick wash plus the vaccine stop rabies before it can start.

impact.cause: A dog bite or scratch that breaks the skin
impact.mechanism: Rabies virus in dog saliva enters through the wound; dogs are the source of almost every human case
impact.effect: Avoiding bites is the main way to avoid rabies, which is nearly always fatal once it begins

magnitude.unit: % of human rabies cases come from dog bites and scratches

impact.supported_by: Dog bites and scratches cause 99% of the human rabies cases, and can be prevented through dog vaccination and bite prevention.
impact.caveat: A global figure that holds in India, which carries one of the world's largest shares of rabies deaths.

impact.cause: Washing a bite wound with soap and running water straight away
impact.mechanism: Prompt, thorough washing removes and inactivates the virus at the wound before it can spread
impact.effect: A simple first-aid step that lowers the chance a bite turns into rabies
impact.supported_by: extensive wound washing with water and soap for at least 15 minutes soon after an exposure
impact.caveat: Washing is first aid, not a substitute for the anti-rabies vaccine after a bite - always see a doctor.

impact.cause: Teasing, chasing, cornering, or stoning a dog
impact.mechanism: A frightened or cornered dog bites to defend itself; these bites are provoked and avoidable
impact.effect: Giving dogs space prevents a real share of bites
impact.source_title: Epidemiological study of animal bite victims in Central India: a cross sectional institutional study
impact.publisher: International Journal of Community Medicine and Public Health (IJCMPH)
impact.caveat: A single-site Indian study; if about three-quarters of bites were unprovoked, roughly a quarter were provoked and preventable.

closer: You couldn't have known. Now you do.

title: Vaccinate your pet. Never dump it.
norm: Get your pet its yearly anti-rabies shot, and never abandon an animal on the street.
wit: A dog you take home is a promise, not a phase.
goesRight: Fewer unvaccinated animals roam free, the virus loses its foothold, and both pets and people are protected - cheaply.

impact.cause: Owners vaccinating their animals against rabies
impact.mechanism: Immunising dogs stops rabies at its animal source, before it can ever reach a person
impact.effect: Vaccinating dogs is the cheapest and most effective way to prevent human rabies
impact.supported_by: Vaccinating dogs, including puppies, through mass dog vaccination programs is the most cost-effective strategy for preventing rabies in people because it stops the transmission at its source.

impact.cause: Pets left unvaccinated, or abandoned to roam free
impact.mechanism: Free-roaming dogs with no vaccination keep the rabies virus circulating
impact.effect: In studies of rabid dogs, the animals were free-roaming and unvaccinated
impact.source_title: First report on dog bite epidemiology and Rabies diagnosis in stray dogs: a one health study from Puducherry
impact.supported_by: All the affected dogs were found to be free-roaming dogs and lacked a proper vaccination record.
impact.caveat: An observational study from one Indian territory; it shows association, not a measured national effect.

closer: You couldn't have known. Now you do.

title: Sterilise and return. Don't kill.
norm: Support humane Animal Birth Control - sterilise, vaccinate, and return street dogs - instead of killing, dumping, or driving them out.
wit: Empty a street of dogs and new ones move in by evening. The map hates a vacuum.
goesRight: Dog numbers ease down gently, more dogs get vaccinated, and streets grow safer - without cruelty or wasted effort.

impact.cause: Killing or driving out free-roaming dogs to cut their numbers
impact.mechanism: Removed dogs are quickly replaced by others from around, so culling does not lower rabies
impact.effect: Culling is not an effective way to control rabies; sterilise-and-vaccinate is
impact.supported_by: Culling free roaming dogs is not effective in controlling rabies.
impact.caveat: India's Animal Birth Control Rules, 2023 set out the humane sterilise-vaccinate-return approach as the lawful method.

closer: You couldn't have known. Now you do.

title: Don't feed the monkeys.
norm: Don't feed monkeys, and keep food and rubbish out of sight, so they stay wild and wary of people.
wit: Feed one monkey today, negotiate with forty tomorrow.
goesRight: Monkeys that keep their natural fear of people forage in the wild, and bites, break-ins and standoffs stay rare.

impact.cause: People feeding monkeys leftovers or fruit
impact.mechanism: Fed monkeys grow used to people, lose their fear, and start to expect food; a refusal can turn into aggression
impact.effect: Feeding sets off a cycle that ends in bold, aggressive troops
impact.source_title: Why Feeding Monkeys is Bad for Forests
impact.supported_by: macaques get accustomed to being fed, lose their fear of humans, and actively seek anthropogenic food. If people refuse to feed them, the macaques can get aggressive.
impact.caveat: Expert commentary describing the behavioural mechanism; the scale of conflict is not quantified here.

impact.cause: Monkeys becoming dependent on human food
impact.mechanism: Habituated troops snatch food and break into buildings when it isn't handed over
impact.effect: Snatching and home break-ins - the everyday face of human-monkey conflict
impact.source_title: Urban Menace: Focus on human-monkey conflict management
impact.publisher: Down To Earth (Centre for Science and Environment)
impact.supported_by: macaques start snatching food from residents when not provided voluntarily, or they start breaking into homes to steal a meal.

closer: You couldn't have known. Now you do.

title: Your dog. Your bag. Your job.
norm: Carry a bag and pick up after your dog. Faeces left on the path spread worms and germs - most of all to the children who play there.
wit: Nobody enjoys the surprise underfoot. Least of all on a good shoe.
goesRight: A quick scoop keeps the park a place children can sit and roll around in without picking up worms.

impact.cause: Dog faeces left in public spaces
impact.mechanism: Toxocara canis roundworms in the dog's gut shed eggs in the faeces; the eggs mature in soil and then infect people through the faecal-oral route, from contaminated soil and hands
impact.effect: Human toxocariasis, with children most at risk
impact.supported_by: Children are more prone to infection via the fecal-oral route as they are more likely to consume Toxocara eggs by ingesting soil or other contaminated substances.
impact.caveat: The transmission route for Toxocara (the dog roundworm) - a global biological mechanism rather than a measured Indian figure. A peer-reviewed medical reference is cited here.

closer: You couldn't have known. Now you do.

title: A cow can't tell it's plastic.
norm: Tie up food waste so free-roaming cows and dogs can't swallow the plastic with it. Plastic they can't digest piles up inside them and slowly makes them sick.
wit: The cow smells last night's dinner. It cannot smell the bag wrapped around it.
goesRight: Food scraps tied off and binned mean the street cow eats fodder, not a bag that will sit in its stomach for years.

impact.cause: Food waste littered in plastic bags where free-roaming cattle graze
impact.mechanism: Cattle swallow plastic while grazing on garbage; plastic cannot be digested or passed in dung, so it accumulates in the rumen and causes impaction, obstruction and loss of appetite
impact.effect: Ruminal impaction in free-roaming cattle, leaving them unable to feed and severely ill
impact.source_title: Ruminal impaction due to plastic materials - an increasing threat to ruminants and its impact on human health in developing countries
impact.supported_by: Animals in these areas graze on plastic garbage leading to the ingestion of plastic waste materials and development of ruminal impaction due to plastic materials.
impact.caveat: A peer-reviewed veterinary review of stray and free-roaming ruminants across developing countries, India among them; no clean measured figure is printed, and it describes impaction and illness rather than a verified cause of death.

closer: You couldn't have known. Now you do.
